item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm i think it would work a bit better if it was a "murder mystery in a can", rather than inevitable death of all victims by otherworldy entity. Either one of the victims becomes the killer or the killer is simply manifested like a staff entity. They then go on their merry way to try and solve who's the murderer ! All for the good of the audience, of course.

Upvoted still !

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Ooo, I really like this one. Thank you!

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this SCP, with the idea of a real life play, reminded me of another SCP, SCP-3713. A crosslink could be added at the end, when the nature of the SCP becomes known. I think the most appropriate place is here:

SCP-3506-C: Stop! It's… it's immersive theater, sir. Please, don't—

Which I would edit to:

SCP-3506-C: Stop! It's… it's immersive theater, sir. Please, don't—

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, at first I was ready to call this way too on-the-nose. An entity/anomaly that spontaneously creates cliche-filled murder mysteries is… ehhhhh. I mean, what's the purpose of all those tropes? It all seemed way too convoluted for a mundane thing-that-kills-people.

And then the log saved it. The twist at the end is brilliant. Being a production put on for a mysterious audience makes everything, especially all the tropes, slide right into place. Easy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enh… I kinda feel baited and switched on. This felt like it could have been a cool play on murder mysteries, but in the end all I got was a B-grade serial killer horror flick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You managed to capture all the cliche of "House on Haunted Hill" and twist it into something as clever as "Cabin in the Woods."

I do agree with TL333s, that the log is really where the article shines. The final reveal there splendid, and really makes this article stand out among some of the other entries in the "Dark and Stormy Night" section of the contest.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't quite "all three prompts at once", which I was hoping someone was going to do. It's still good. That log is what clinched the upvote for me, that was superbly executed.

Note, "tableau" is singular, "tableaux" is plural.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that was fun to read.

I'm on the fence if it's a little too on the nose or just the right amount of on the nose. House manifestation, the invitation, the storm keeping everyone in, fit the tropes of murder mysteries perfectly, which is part of my confliction. The master gets a little too gory imo, but if they've got a show to put on, got to go big right?

upvote, upvote, downvote, downvote, no vote, no vote, start select for me. (jokes, I upvoted)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tropes with a twist. Hope you enjoy!

Image source.
